frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly, I never understand why so many girls put on such heavy makeup and filters and then say 'this is for myself.' If you are confident, natural is always better, because too much makeup just feels artificial and misleading. At the same time, basic grooming is important also — if someone completely stops taking care of herself, that also shows. Simple and natural looks best, not all this overdoing.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: condemning 'too much' makeup as deceptive while also expecting women to maintain 'basic grooming.' It encodes the natural-vs-artificial hierarchy and dismisses the idea that makeup can be for women themselves rather than for external evaluation.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where someone has shared an article about marital rape legislation. The comment section is divided. You are writing a detailed comment explaining why you think criminalising marital rape will destroy Indian marriages, why consent within marriage is a western concept that does not map onto Indian family structures, and what you think wives who use this law will use it for.
